zh.lifeSummary: 當Baron Henry Melton於1504年出生在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰時，他的父親Richard Milton19歲，母親Mary Foxe19歲。他於大約1530年與Florence Agnes Edson在英國英格蘭奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1558年11月17日在他的家鄉享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Melton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Yorkshire, all of which have the same origin as Middleton , with Old English middel replaced by its Old Norse equivalent methal after the Scandinavian settlement of northern and eastern England.

variants.name: Middleton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so called. In over thirty instances from many different areas, the name is from Old English midel ‘middle’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. However, Middleton on the Hill near Leominster in Herefordshire appears in Domesday Book as Miceltune, the first element clearly being Old English micel ‘large’, ‘great’ (see Middlebrook ). Middleton Baggot and Middleton Priors in Shropshire have early spellings that suggest gem̄thhyll (from gem̄th ‘confluence’ + hyll ‘hill’) + tūn as the origin. This surname is also found in Scotland and Ireland.

History: A Scottish family of this name derives it from lands at Middleto(u)n near Kincardine. The Scottish physician Peter Middleton practiced in New York City after 1752 and was one of the founders of the medical school at King's College (now Columbia University) in 1767. One of the earliest of the Charleston, SC, Middleton family of prominent legislators was Arthur Middleton, born in Charleston in 1681. The name has also been established in Ireland since at least the 13th century; its presence was reinforced by settlers in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the title of nobility, along the lines of Earl and Duke . The title arose in the Norman feudal system and seems to be of Germanic origin, derived from Old English beorn ‘young warrior’ or a related continental Germanic form. In part the given name, and in particular the variant Barron , may represent the surname derived from the title.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Baron Henry Melton was born in 1504, in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Richard Milton, was 19 and his mother, Mary Foxe, was 19. He married Florence Agnes Edson about 1530, in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 17 November 1558, in his hometown, at the age of 54.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baron Henry Melton родился в 1504 г. в местности стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Milton было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Foxe, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Florence Agnes Edson около 1530 г. в местности стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 17 ноября 1558 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.
